items.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
items.summary: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.

items.title: Five dead following shooting in Stade, northern Germany
items.summary: Two people have been arrested, police said.

items.title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
items.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

items.title: Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan
items.summary: The Taliban government says civilians were killed, while Pakistan maintains they targeted militants.

items.title: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
items.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

items.title: 'You are Canadian heroes' - the forgotten hosts making World Cup history
items.summary: Canada are making history at this World Cup with a run that is likely to change the face of football in the country forever.

items.title: Teyana Taylor's tears and Lauryn Hill tribute steal show at BET Awards
items.summary: Taylor had an emotional reaction to being presented with the Icon of the Year award by Janet Jackson.

items.title: Fitness brand founder and Olympian Les Mills dies aged 91
items.summary: The Les Mills International brand is now known globally for choreographed workouts set to music, like Body Pump and Body Combat.

items.title: Indian journalists condemn 'denial' of voting and passport rights of prominent editor
items.summary: Rajagopal has alleged that he has been unable to renew his passport after his name was deleted from the electoral rolls.

items.title: Biden blasts Trump 'vanity projects', calling him a 'loser' at fundraising event
items.summary: The former US president took aim at his successor's projects in Washington DC, including a planned new White House ballroom.

items.title: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
items.summary: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

items.title: Senior South African police officer survives assassination attempt
items.summary: Deputy crime intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was due to appear before an inquiry into police corruption this week.

items.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
items.summary: The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.

items.title: Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand
items.summary: The body of Tunchanok Donhomla, 17, was found discarded near a railway track on Saturday, police say.

items.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
items.summary: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.

items.title: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
items.summary: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
items.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

items.title: 117 dead dogs found at California 'no-kill' animal rescue - many with gunshot wounds
items.summary: Hundreds more animals are still missing from the California facility as investigators continue excavating the grounds.

items.title: Uganda's leading media outlets shut down by army chief
items.summary: NTV and Daily Monitor say their offices in the capital are under "military siege".

items.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
items.summary: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.

items.title: Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday
items.summary: The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person

items.title: Heatwave breaks records in Germany, Denmark and Czech Republic
items.summary: An estimated 150 million people have been experiencing temperatures of over 35C across Europe.

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
items.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

items.title: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
items.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

items.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
items.summary: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions

items.summary: As the Netflix show following their lives returns, the squad open up about their new-found fame.

items.title: Loved and loathed: The making of India's viral liver doctor
items.summary: Dr Cyriac Abby Philips has built a large online following, but often clashes with India's traditional medicine specialists.

items.title: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
items.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

items.title: Bad Bunny lights up London with history-making stadium show
items.summary: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.

items.summary: Brown talks to the BBC about having her "hands and heart" all over the detective adventure series.

items.summary: Tidying expert Dilly Carter shares her best tips on organising your kitchen, lounge and clothes.

items.title: BBC at Venezuela baseball stadium sheltering earthquake survivors
items.summary: The survivors are hopeful the authorities will find them some form of temporary accommodation while the country rebuilds - for now they are dependant on donated food and clothing.

items.title: Anger directed at Venezuela's official response to earthquake recovery
items.summary: Residents in quake-affected areas have expressed anger over the state's efforts to locate survivors and help victims, the BBC's Orla Guerin reports.

items.title: Watch: Inside the Venezuela country club now a makeshift hospital
items.summary: Survivors of Venezuela's devastating earthquakes are being cared for at a makeshift hospital formed on the site of a country club in Caraballeda.

items.title: Panic attacks and fractures - Venezuela hospital treats earthquake victims
items.summary: The BBC travelled to a Caracas hospital that is treating people from the worst affected areas.

items.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
items.summary: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.
